name: MLX-LM (Apple Silicon LLM Inference)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Apple; optimized for Apple Silicon M-series chips

auth.notes: Local inference; HuggingFace token for private models

performance.notes: Unified memory on Apple Silicon; 30-80 tokens/sec depending on model and chip; M2/M3/M4 Ultra recommended for 70B models

agent_readiness.notes: LLM inference on Apple Silicon using Apple's MLX framework. Leverages unified memory (CPU+GPU share memory — no VRAM limit). Supports LLaMA, Mistral, Gemma, Phi, Qwen, and any HuggingFace model in MLX format. Convert models with mlx_lm.convert. Generate text with mlx_lm.generate or mlx_lm.server (OpenAI-compatible API). LoRA fine-tuning supported. Performance rivals GPU inference on Mac hardware. macOS only — Apple Silicon required.

gotchas: macOS only — Apple Silicon required (no x86 support)
MLX format requires model conversion from safetensors/GGUF
Community documentation lags behind active development
best_when: Local LLM inference on MacBook/Mac Studio/Mac Pro
Privacy-sensitive workloads requiring no cloud API calls
Development and testing of LLM applications locally
Running 7B-70B models on Apple Silicon without cloud costs
avoid_when: Non-Apple hardware (use llama.cpp, vLLM, or Ollama)
Production serving at scale (use vLLM + cloud GPU)
summary: MLX-LM enables LLM inference on Apple Silicon using Apple's MLX framework and unified memory architecture. No VRAM limits — models run in unified CPU+GPU memory. Supports LLaMA, Mistral, Gemma, Phi, Qwen via HuggingFace MLX models. OpenAI-compatible local server, streaming, and LoRA fine-tuning. Best performance for local LLM on Mac hardware. macOS + Apple Silicon only.
